name: LIP
description:
LIP (Lip Sync) files drive facial lip animation in KotOR I & II. They store a short timeline: a total duration in seconds and a list of keyframes. Each keyframe has a time (in seconds from the start) and a lip shape index (0–15) that picks a phoneme shape (ee, oh, m, etc.). Playback interpolates or steps between keyframes to sync lips to dialogue or sound.

Layout: 16-byte header with magic ("LIP "), version, duration (float, seconds), and entryCount. The keyframe array follows immediately: entryCount entries, 5 bytes each—a float (time) and a byte (shape index). Keyframes are usually sorted by time. The LipShape enum maps shape index to a phoneme name for reference.

Parsing: Read the header; duration gives the clip length, entryCount says how many keyframes. Read entryCount × 5 bytes starting at offset 16. Each keyframe is time (4 bytes, float) then shape (1 byte, u8). Use the LipShape enum to interpret shape (e.g. 0 = ee_teeth, 11 = m_p_b).

Writing: Choose duration and build the keyframe list (time in seconds, shape 0–15). Sort by time. Write header (fileType, fileVersion, duration, entryCount), then each keyframe as 4-byte float + 1-byte shape. All multi-byte values are little-endian.

Header.description: 16 bytes at the start. fileType is "LIP ", fileVersion is the format version. duration is the total length of the animation in seconds. entryCount is how many keyframes follow; the keyframe block is entryCount × 5 bytes starting at offset 16.

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Total duration of the lip animation in seconds.

fields.name: entryCount
fields.description: Number of keyframes. Keyframe block size = entryCount × 5 bytes.

KeyFrame.description: One keyframe (5 bytes): time in seconds from the start of the clip, and a lip shape index (0–15). See LipShape for the meaning of each index. Keyframes are typically ordered by time.

fields.name: time
fields.description: Time in seconds (from start of clip) when this shape is shown.

fields.name: shape
fields.description: Lip shape index 0–15; see LipShape enum.

LipShape.description: Lip shape / phoneme index (0–15). Standard set used for lip-sync: each value corresponds to a mouth shape (e.g. ee, oh, m). Use this to interpret the shape byte in each keyframe.

sections.name: header
sections.description: Fixed 16-byte header. Gives duration and entryCount; keyframes start at byte 16.

sections.name: keyframes
sections.description: Keyframe array: entryCount entries, 5 bytes each (f32 time, u8 shape). Read from offset 16. Entry i is at 16 + i×5. Interpret shape with the LipShape enum.
